input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
The most likely cause is that your page's **`BindingContext`** is never set, so the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

**Fix — set the BindingContext** (pick one approach):

**In code-behind (`MainPage.xaml.cs`):**
```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Or in XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="YourApp.MainPage">
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

**Also verify** your ViewModel raises change notifications — either:
- Inherit from `ObservableObject` (MVVM Toolkit), or
- Implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` and fire `PropertyChanged` in each setter.

```csharp
// Simplest approach using MVVM Community Toolkit:
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    [ObservableProperty]
    private string title = "Hello";

    [ObservableProperty]
    private string description = "World";
}
```

**TL;DR:** No `BindingContext` → bindings silently resolve to `null` → blank labels.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values. Looking at their XAML, the bindings look correct syntactically, but there are two likely issues:

1. **BindingContext is not set** — The most common reason. The page needs to know which ViewModel instance to bind against.
2. **ViewModel doesn't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`** — If properties are set after the binding context is assigned, the UI won't update without property change notifications.

The XAML itself doesn't show a `BindingContext` being set, which is the most likely cause.
